segments.text: The most important thing which you can do in a bull market, the single most important thing, is to remain bullish.
Prematurely becoming bearish is a big problem in a bull market.
You need to be bullish and need to remain bullish for months, years till there is real deterioration.
What is going to happen is first set of stocks are going to rally.
They're going to get like, an end.
Then you can start thinking, oh, no.
This rally is over.
No.
This rally is not going to be over.
This rally is going to last.
So remaining bullish and everyday coming in and telling yourself, look, I need to look for bullish setups.
Because there'll be bullish setup, but just like in a you start fee and this is what happened in the first time I, like, went into a bullish market.
I thought, like, never.
This is like in a really big big money now, like, in a this is over.
But then again, second and third wave of stocks keep breaking out.
And that is the key to remaining bullish.
If you remember, like, and if you read Livermore's book and in which there is that character who is that turkey guy, whatever his name, the old turkey, and he keeps saying, look at it.
It's a bull market.
It's a bull market.
It's a bull market.
Till you get tired, you have to remember that it is a bull market.
I've seen this problem again and again.
I was smart enough to recognize it in in the first year, bull market in 2001, 2002.
And, there are, like, a lot of people who said, like, you know, the stock has made market has made a big move, and it's become a bubble.
Just like in a just because the market has gone up a big in one year doesn't make it a bubble.
Bubble happens after ten years, fifteen years.
It's when lot of new people have come into the market.
Retail traders have taken over the market.

So remember, this is very, very, very important that to remain bullish in a bullish market sounds like a easy thing, but it is not so easy because you're conditioned by the bearish market to think in terms of short term.
You're going to get out of your positions.
But like that damn thing, kept on going up for four years.
Right?

And here, the market actually kept on going up for thirteen, fourteen years till you had a significant, like, an eleven years till you had a so there is the tendency to become bearish in a bullish market because you think, look, and I the stocks are made big moves up.
There is bubble.
The bubble is like an it's going to blow up, and it is going to become a big bubble till that time.
It's tight time to be bullish.
And this is very important because, two things can happen.

One is like in a in 02/2000, 2001, 2001, or 2008, 2009, when the market especially 2008, 2009, I saw a lot of people started shutting the market because they were still conditioned to think, which is the same thing if you see currently.
A Lot of people are saying, oh, the market is going to collapse.
The economy is going to collapse.
They're never going to come back.
This is Japan.
This is this.
This is that.
Right?
So as a result, when it started rallying, big losses people made because they're conditioned to short.
And they never believed in the rally, and the rally kept on going up.
So in bull market, remaining bullish sounds like a very thing, but it's the most difficult task because there is always people who are telling you, look, this has become a bubble.
That stock is like this has happened.
That has happened.
Oh, they are just, like, in a pumping in money like crazy.
Yeah.
But that's, like, not what your job is.
Your job is to remain bullish for three, four, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten, eleven, twelve, thirteen years.
Because look at just the market.
That's how the market operates.
Right?
And if you see the market, when it goes up, it goes up.
Like here, if you see, after, like, in a brief correction in '89 or '90.
Right?
The market kept going up for three years.
And after that, there's a brief pause and correction, and then the market kept going up.
But others, like, in a almost like in a, five, six, seven, or eight, nine years.
And this is what is going to be the big cycles which are going to last.
Some of the big cycles are going to last twenty to thirty, forty years.
And if you continuously become bearish, then you are going to miss out on a lot of big moves.
Yes.
Certain stocks are going to get, like, in a very extended.
They are going to get, like, in a way over the top kind of a thing.
They're then you are going to feel like a look and they are like an explode.
But always look for the new thing.
Because when stocks start like, Wayfair and all stop going up, you'll see that those stocks, newer IPOs, newer stock, UPST made a big move.
Right?
And it came out of the gate, made a big move.
You had a firm making a big move.
So the it's like a relay race.
Right?
Fresh set of stocks come into the market during bull market.
They make big moves.
Maybe you may not believe, like, why they should make big moves.
That's a different story, But they make big moves.
Another set of stocks make big moves.
Another set of stocks make big moves.
But if you become prematurely bearish, you're gonna miss out on a lot of opportunities.
So it might sound very stupid, but the thing to do in a bull market is to remain bullish as long as possible till you really see a deterioration.
And if you can do that, you can make a lot of money in a bull market.
